name: ochd expander
description: 4HP expander for ochd ([ø]4^2). Adds 16 outputs in four groups derived from ochd's 8 LFOs: full-wave-rectified unipolar LFOs, analog AND/OR diode logic pairs, cascading stochastic triggers, and R-2R 4-bit slow-noise DACs. Connects via two 2x4 IDC expander cables.

outputs.name: f.2 (LFO 1 rectified)
outputs.description: Full-wave-rectified LFO 1. Negative half of the triangle is flipped positive — unipolar triangle at 2× the original rate. Top-left jack of the f.2 group.

outputs.name: f.2 (LFO 3 rectified)
outputs.description: Full-wave-rectified LFO 3. Top-right jack of the f.2 group.

outputs.name: f.2 (LFO 5 rectified)
outputs.description: Full-wave-rectified LFO 5. Bottom-left jack of the f.2 group.

outputs.name: f.2 (LFO 7 rectified)
outputs.description: Full-wave-rectified LFO 7. Bottom-right jack of the f.2 group.

outputs.name: + (LFO 2 OR LFO 3)
outputs.description: Analog diode OR (max voltage) of LFO 2 and LFO 3. Top-left jack of the +/- group.

outputs.name: − (LFO 2 AND LFO 3)
outputs.description: Analog diode AND (min voltage) of LFO 2 and LFO 3. Bottom-left jack of the +/- group.

outputs.name: + (LFO 6 OR LFO 7)
outputs.description: Analog diode OR (max voltage) of LFO 6 and LFO 7. Top-right jack of the +/- group.

outputs.name: − (LFO 6 AND LFO 7)
outputs.description: Analog diode AND (min voltage) of LFO 6 and LFO 7. Bottom-right jack of the +/- group.

outputs.name: Trig (LFO 2)
outputs.description: ~8ms trigger at the start of each LFO 2 rising edge. Top-left jack of the Trig group. Normalled clockwise into the next Trig jack — leave unpatched to layer into the LFO 4 trigger output.

outputs.name: Trig (LFO 4 / +LFO 2)
outputs.description: Top-right Trig jack. With top-left Trig unpatched, sums LFO 2 + LFO 4 triggers; with top-left patched, fires on LFO 4 only.

outputs.name: Trig (LFO 6 / +prior)
outputs.description: Bottom-right Trig jack. Layers LFO 2 + LFO 4 + LFO 6 triggers when the two top Trig jacks are unpatched; collapses to LFO 6 only when prior jacks are patched.

outputs.name: Trig (LFO 8 / +prior)
outputs.description: Bottom-left Trig jack — final stage of the cascade. Layers LFO 2 + LFO 4 + LFO 6 + LFO 8 triggers when all prior Trig jacks are unpatched; collapses to LFO 8 only when all three prior jacks are patched.

outputs.name: R²R (LFOs 1–4 DAC)
outputs.description: R-2R 4-bit DAC fed by LFOs 1, 2, 3, 4 (LFO 1 = MSB, LFO 4 = LSB). Stepped slow-noise voltage. Top-left jack of the R²R group.

outputs.name: R²R (LFOs 5–8 DAC)
outputs.description: R-2R 4-bit DAC fed by LFOs 5, 6, 7, 8 (LFO 5 = MSB, LFO 8 = LSB). Slower slow-noise variant. Top-right jack of the R²R group.

outputs.name: R²R (odd LFOs 1,3,5,7 DAC)
outputs.description: R-2R 4-bit DAC fed by odd LFOs (LFO 1 MSB → LFO 7 LSB). Bottom-left jack of the R²R group.

outputs.name: R²R (even LFOs 2,4,6,8 DAC)
outputs.description: R-2R 4-bit DAC fed by even LFOs (LFO 2 MSB → LFO 8 LSB). Bottom-right jack of the R²R group.

behaviors.name: Full-wave rectification (f.2)
behaviors.description: The four f.2 outputs flip the negative half of LFOs 1, 3, 5, 7 positive. Result: unipolar (0–5V) triangles at twice the original rate — useful for envelopes, attack-only modulation, or drum decays.

behaviors.name: Analog AND / OR diode logic
behaviors.description: Each + jack outputs the higher of its two LFO inputs (OR / max); each − jack outputs the lower (AND / min). Output is bipolar ±5V. Acts as a max/min mixer that creates new compound shapes from LFO pairs (2&3, 6&7).

behaviors.name: Cascading trigger normalling
behaviors.trigger: leave Trig jacks unpatched to layer
behaviors.description: The four Trig outputs are normalled clockwise (top-left → top-right → bottom-right → bottom-left). Each jack sums its own LFO trigger with the previous unpatched jacks' triggers. Tap the bottom-left Trig with all others unpatched to get a four-LFO stochastic trigger stream; patch a Trig jack to break the cascade at that point.

behaviors.name: R-2R 4-bit slow noise
behaviors.description: Four R-2R ladder DACs treat groups of four LFOs as 4-bit binary words. Output is a 16-step stepped CV. Step rate and shape depend on the bit-ordering: LFOs 1–4 is fastest, 5–8 slowest, odd and even groups give two different chaotic flavours.

behaviors.name: All outputs follow ochd's Rate
behaviors.description: There are no controls on the expander itself. Every output speeds up or slows down with ochd's master Rate knob and Rate CV input — including the R²R noise step rate and the trigger density.

patchIdeas.name: Push-pull modulation
patchIdeas.patch: f.2 LFO 3 (unipolar 2× rate) → VCF cutoff · ochd LFO 3 → VCA CV. Filter pumps at double rate against the original LFO on the VCA.
steps: Patch ochd LFO 3 → VCA CV.
Patch ochd expander f.2 (LFO 3 rectified) → VCF cutoff input.
Set ochd Rate knob to noon so LFO 3 cycles roughly once every few seconds.
Send a VCO through the VCF then the VCA; monitor the VCA output.
Listen: each LFO 3 swell on the VCA is crossed by two filter sweeps from the rectified output (it runs at 2× rate).
Turn ochd Rate slowly CW to speed both up together — the 1:2 ratio stays locked.
patchIdeas.signalOut: VCA out — audio whose amplitude follows ochd LFO 3 (±5V) while the filter sweeps at 2× rate (0–5V from f.2).
patchIdeas.hear: A pulsing tone where every amplitude swell contains two distinct filter wah-wahs. Pure ochd alone would give one filter sweep per swell — the rectifier doubles it.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ochd expander:
- ochd (LFO 3) >> VCA (CV)
- ochd expander (f.2 LFO 3) >> VCF (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> VCF (In)
- VCF (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stochastic drum triggers from one Trig jack
patchIdeas.patch: Tap the bottom-left Trig jack with all other Trig jacks unpatched — get LFO 2 + 4 + 6 + 8 triggers layered into one cascading rhythm into a drum module.
steps: Leave the top-left, top-right, and bottom-right Trig jacks unpatched (this lets the cascade normalise through).
Patch ochd expander Trig (LFO 8 / +prior, the bottom-left jack) → drum module trigger input.
Set ochd Rate knob to noon.
Listen: triggers from LFOs 2, 4, 6, and 8 all layer into the drum — fast hits from LFO 2, slower hits from LFO 8.
Now patch the top-left Trig (LFO 2) → a hi-hat trigger input. The bottom-left jack instantly drops LFO 2's contribution and only LFOs 4, 6, 8 remain there.
Patch the top-right Trig too → a clap trigger. Bottom-left now only fires on LFO 6 + LFO 8.
Sweep ochd Rate knob to thicken or thin the rhythm globally.
patchIdeas.signalOut: ochd expander Trig (LFO 8) bottom-left jack — ~8ms 5V triggers, layered count depends on how many prior Trig jacks are unpatched.
patchIdeas.hear: An evolving stochastic kick/hat pattern. Patching/unpatching the upper Trig jacks subtracts or adds layers in real time — a hands-on way to thin out a busy rhythm.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ochd expander:
- ochd expander (Trig LFO 8) g> Drum (Trig) // cascade sums LFO 2+4+6+8 when prior jacks unpatched
- ochd expander (Trig LFO 2) g> HiHat (Trig) // breaks LFO 2 out of the cascade
- ochd expander (Trig LFO 4) g> Clap (Trig) // breaks LFO 4 out

patchIdeas.name: Stepped slow-noise pitch sequence
patchIdeas.patch: R²R (LFOs 1–4) → quantizer → VCO 1V/Oct. Sixteen-step random pitch melody whose step rate follows ochd's Rate knob.
steps: Patch ochd expander R²R (LFOs 1–4 DAC) → quantizer CV input.
Patch quantizer CV out → VCO 1V/Oct input.
Choose a scale on the quantizer (e.g. minor pentatonic).
Set ochd Rate knob to noon — you'll hear a slowly evolving 16-step pitch sequence.
Patch one of the Trig outputs (e.g. Trig LFO 4) → quantizer trigger / clock-in so notes lock to the trigger grid.
Send the VCO through a VCA gated by the same Trig output for plucks.
Try R²R (LFOs 5–8) for slower pitch drift, or R²R (odd) / R²R (even) for different flavours of chaos.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Quantizer out — stepped pitch CV from the R-2R DAC, retriggered by an expander Trig output.
patchIdeas.hear: A generative 16-step melody that never quite repeats. Switching to the LFOs 5–8 R²R output makes the melody slow to a near-drone; the odd/even DAC outputs give pricklier, more chaotic sequences.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ochd expander:
- ochd expander (R²R LFOs 1–4) >> Quantizer (CV In)
- Quantizer (CV Out) >> VCO (1V/Oct)
- ochd expander (Trig LFO 4) g> Quantizer (Clock)
- ochd expander (Trig LFO 4) g> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
